name: Gene Lees
description: Canadian music critic, biographer, lyricist, and journalist (1928–2010)
abstract: Frederick Eugene John Lees (February 8, 1928 – April 22, 2010) was a Canadian music critic, biographer, lyricist, and journalist. Lees worked as a newspaper journalist in his native Canada before moving to the United States, where he was a music critic and lyricist.

about_quotes.speaker: Warren Bernhardt
about_quotes.evidence: Gene Lees, who was the editor at Down Beat and he was a good friend and he was a songwriter and a singer and I’d accompanied him in Chicago and in the Midwest.
about_quotes.doc_title: Warren Bernhardt interviewed by Monk Rowe, Bearsville, New York, January 3, 2012

about_quotes.speaker: Art Farmer
about_quotes.evidence: One time, I was talking to this writer named Gene Lees about this thing, and Gene has a sister in Canada who is married to a brain specialist, so Gene told me, “You ought to have a test because you and your brother are twins, and whatever was going on with him maybe is going on with you too.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Art Farmer — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Crow
about_quotes.evidence: I knew Gene Lees who had been the editor of Down Beat for quite a while... during that period I’d gotten to know him because he was hanging around Jim & Andy’s in New York a lot
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Crow interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, October 18, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Hentoff Nat
about_quotes.evidence: Later I found out, from Gene Lees I guess, who is the historian of the Woody Herman band, among others, that she had more stamina than they.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hentoff Nat — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Brubeck
about_quotes.evidence: who Gene Lees is? ... Gene was doing an interview with Dave and he was asking him how did you get through World War II

about_quotes.speaker: Orrin Keepnews
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] You were, according to Gene Lees, I believe said you were the biggest Bill Evans nut he ever knew.
about_quotes.doc_title: Orrin Keepnews interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Francisco, California, August 7, 2002
